content: Hi, I'm Dana Moore, and I'm a wedding planner. I've been working on a project to make my business more sustainable, and I'm excited to share some of my ideas.
That's great to hear, Dana. What inspired you to focus on sustainability in your business?

I've always been passionate about the environment, and I realized that the wedding industry has a significant impact on waste and carbon emissions. I've been working with my clients to reduce waste and choose more eco-friendly options, and it's been a really rewarding experience.

That's a wonderful approach, Dana. Reducing waste and promoting sustainability in the wedding industry can make a big difference. Have you considered offering any eco-friendly wedding packages or services?

Yes, I've been developing some new packages that focus on sustainability, and I'm excited to launch them soon. I've also been working on an online course to teach other wedding planners about sustainable practices, and it's been a fun project.
That's a great idea, Dana. Sharing your knowledge and expertise with others can help create a more sustainable industry. What topics will you be covering in your course?

I'll be covering everything from sustainable venue choices to eco-friendly decor and catering options. I've also been learning about zero-waste weddings and how to reduce waste in the planning process.

That sounds like a comprehensive course, Dana. I'm sure it will be really helpful for wedding planners who want to make a positive impact. Do you have any favorite sustainable wedding trends or ideas that you've come across?

I actually really love the idea of using locally sourced and seasonal flowers, and I've been working with some amazing florists who share my vision. I've also been enjoying knitting and surfing in my free time, and it's been a great way to relax and express my creativity.
Knitting and surfing are great hobbies, Dana. They can be so therapeutic and fulfilling. Do you have a favorite type of yarn or surf spot that you enjoy?
I actually really love working with wool yarn, and I've been trying to create some sustainable knitwear pieces. I've also been surfing in California, and it's been an amazing experience.
That sounds wonderful, Dana. California has some beautiful surf spots. Have you considered sharing your sustainable knitwear designs or surfing adventures with others on social media or a blog?
I've thought about it, but I'm not sure I have the time. I've been so busy with work and my online course. But I do love sharing my passion for sustainability and creativity with others.

I completely understand, Dana. It can be tough to balance multiple passions and responsibilities. Maybe you could start small and share some of your designs or adventures with your friends and family on social media?
That's a great idea. I'll have to think about it some more. By the way, I've been trying to get into travel blogging, and I've been writing about some of my favorite sustainable travel destinations.

Travel blogging is a great way to share your experiences and inspire others to travel sustainably, Dana. Do you have a favorite travel destination or a favorite type of sustainable tourism that you enjoy?
